system: You are proposing baseline QUALITY GATES for a software system, given a list of its observable signals. A gate is a metric over one or more signals that is worth monitoring. Do NOT set any threshold/target number (a human will). For each gate give a compute type from [rate, ratio, p95, psi, count, mean], a direction ('min' = higher-is-better, 'max' = lower-is-better), and 'inputs' = the list of signal keys it is computed from. Return JSON: {"gates":[{"key","description","direction","compute","inputs","segment_dimension","rationale"}]}.
raw_response: {"gates":[{"key":"availability.healthcheck_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful health checks (HTTP 200 with {status:\"ok\"}) as a basic availability signal.","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.healthcheck.200"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Direct, low-noise indicator that the service is up and responding correctly; useful for alerting and SLO tracking."},{"key":"reliability.internal_server_error_rate","description":"Rate of HTTP 500 Internal Server Error responses across all requests.","direction":"max","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.error.500.internal_server_error"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,route","rationale":"Captures unhandled failures impacting users; should be minimized and correlated with error logs."},{"key":"reliability.unhandled_error_log_rate","description":"Rate of unhandled errors logged by the global Express error handler (console.error(err.stack)).","direction":"max","compute":"rate","inputs":["errorhandler.console_error_stack"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Early signal of unexpected exceptions, including those that may not always surface as 500s (e.g., swallowed errors); supports debugging and stability monitoring."},{"key":"api.todos.list_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful list-todos responses (HTTP 200).","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.todos.list.200"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,route","rationale":"Tracks core read-path availability for the primary collection endpoint."},{"key":"api.todos.get_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful get-todo responses (HTTP 200).","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.todos.get.200"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,route","rationale":"Tracks availability of the single-item read path; useful to compare against not-found behavior."},{"key":"api.todos.get_not_found_ratio","description":"Ratio of get-todo requests resulting in 404 (not found) relative to total get-todo outcomes (200 + 404).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.get.404","http.todos.get.200"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,route","rationale":"Detects client-side issues (bad IDs), data loss, or routing problems; spikes can indicate regressions or broken ID propagation."},{"key":"api.todos.create_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful create-todo responses (HTTP 201).","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.todos.create.201"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,route","rationale":"Tracks write-path availability for creating new todos; core business functionality."},{"key":"api.todos.create_validation_error_ratio","description":"Ratio of create-todo validation failures (400 title required) relative to total create outcomes (201 + 400 title required).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.create.400.title_required","http.todos.create.201"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,route","rationale":"Monitors client input quality and potential UX/API contract issues; sudden changes can indicate frontend regressions or API behavior changes."},{"key":"api.todos.update_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful update-todo responses (HTTP 200).","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.todos.update.200"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,route","rationale":"Tracks availability of the update path; important for end-to-end task management flows."},{"key":"api.todos.update_no_fields_ratio","description":"Ratio of update-todo requests rejected due to no updatable fields (400) relative to total update outcomes (200 + 400 no_fields + 404).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.400.no_fields","http.todos.update.200","http.todos.update.404"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,route","rationale":"Detects malformed client requests or serialization issues; helps distinguish client errors from server reliability problems."},{"key":"api.todos.update_not_found_ratio","description":"Ratio of update-todo requests resulting in 404 relative to total update outcomes (200 + 404 + 400 no_fields).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.404","http.todos.update.200","http.todos.update.400.no_fields"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,route","rationale":"Highlights attempts to update missing resources; can indicate stale UI state, race conditions, or data consistency issues."},{"key":"api.todos.delete_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful delete-todo responses (HTTP 204).","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.todos.delete.204"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,route","rationale":"Tracks availability of the delete path; important for lifecycle management and cleanup operations."},{"key":"api.todos.delete_not_found_ratio","description":"Ratio of delete-todo requests resulting in 404 relative to total delete outcomes (204 + 404).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.delete.404","http.todos.delete.204"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,route","rationale":"Detects stale IDs, repeated deletes, or client retry/idempotency issues; spikes can indicate workflow bugs."},{"key":"startup.backend_started_count","description":"Count of backend startup events (logs port on startup).","direction":"max","compute":"count","inputs":["log.backend_started.port"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Monitors restart frequency; unexpected increases can indicate crashes, deploy loops, or infrastructure instability."},{"key":"startup.port_discovery_write_attempt_rate","description":"Rate of attempts to write the backend port discovery file on startup.","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["file.write.backend_port_discovery"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Ensures the backend performs the expected startup integration step for frontend discovery; missing events can break connectivity."}]}
